Title: RrMYB5- and RrMYB10-regulated flavonoid biosynthesis plays a pivotal role in feedback loop responding to wounding and oxidation in Rosa rugosa

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the flavonoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves; petals; shoots; somatic embryos; tobacco flowers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant defense and enhanced tolerance to wounding, oxidative stress, and dehydration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression promotes proanthocyanidin accumulation and enhances oxidative stress tolerance; transcriptomic effects overlap with RrMYB10 and RrANR transgenics.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in tobacco; RNA-seq comparison with control; metabolite profiling; stress tolerance assays.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RrDFR expression increased in wounded and oxidized rose tissues together with PA accumulation. 2. Transcriptomic profiling of RrDFR-overexpressing tobacco showed altered flavonoid, hormone, and antioxidant-related genes. 3. RrDFR overexpression enhanced stress tolerance and was used as a pathway comparator supporting the RrMYB10-to-PA regulatory model.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: wounding; methyl viologen-induced oxidation; H2O2 treatment; dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dramatically induced in wounded leaves and petals; induced during methyl viologen treatment; expressed in roots, stems, leaves, and flowers.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NADPH-dependent oxidoreductase of the DFR family
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway gene for increasing PA accumulation and oxidative stress tolerance, potentially in combination with upstream MYB regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May shift flavonoid flux away from alternative branches; detailed yield or growth penalties not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as a validated downstream structural gene and transgenic comparator in this study rather than the principal discovery target.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to flavan-3-ols that serve as building blocks for proanthocyanidins.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves; petals; shoots; somatic embryos; tobacco flowers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant defense and enhanced tolerance to wounding, oxidative stress, and dehydration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases PA accumulation and enhances oxidative/dehydration stress tolerance; serves as a major downstream effector of RrMYB10 and RrMYB5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previous and current overexpression evidence in tobacco; RNA-seq comparison; qRT-PCR as downstream target; dual-luciferase promoter activation; metabolite profiling; stress assays.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RrANR expression increased in wounded and oxidized rose tissues with PA accumulation. 2. RrMYB5 and RrMYB10 activated the RrANR promoter in dual-luciferase assays. 3. RrANR overexpression in tobacco enhanced PA accumulation and stress tolerance, and transcriptomic tendencies overlapped strongly with RrMYB10 transgenics.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: wounding; methyl viologen-induced oxidation; H2O2 treatment; dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dramatically induced in wounded tissues; induced during methyl viologen treatment; highly expressed in stems and roots.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NADPH-dependent reductase of the ANR/BAN family
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering PA accumulation and oxidative stress tolerance, either alone or stacked with upstream MYB activators.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May redirect flux from anthocyanin to PA branch; broader agronomic penalties not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly concludes that RrMYB10 is an upstream regulator of RrANR based on expression, promoter activation, and transcriptome concordance.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rose leaves; petals; shoots; somatic embryos; tobacco flowers
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant protection and enhanced tolerance to wounding, oxidative stress, and dehydration.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis, especially proanthocyanidin branch activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; mutual activation with RrMYB10; combinatorial regulation with bHLH partner
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: RrMYB10; RrDFR; RrLAR; RrANR; flavonoid structural genes including CHS, CHI, F3H, F3'H/F3OH, ANS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates flavonoid structural genes and RrMYB10; up-regulates PA biosynthetic genes, especially LAR and ANR-associated branch genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major PA regulator induced by wounding and oxidation; promotes PA accumulation more effectively than typical PA1-type MYBs noted by authors.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression strongly increases proanthocyanidin accumulation in rose and tobacco; in tobacco it increases PA but decreases anthocyanin, altering flower color; improves oxidative and dehydration tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR induction analysis under wounding/MV/light; overexpression in rose and tobacco; transient expression in rose petals; dual-luciferase promoter assays; yeast two-hybrid; split luciferase complementation; metabolite profiling; stress assays.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RrMYB5 is a PA1-type MYB highly induced by wounding and methyl viologen oxidation, with expression patterns matching PA accumulation. 2. It localizes to the nucleus, physically interacts with RrMYB10 and AtEGL3, and activates promoters of RrMYB10, RrDFR, RrLAR and RrANR. 3. Overexpression in rose and tobacco enhances PA accumulation and stress tolerance, while in tobacco anthocyanin levels decline, indicating branch-flux reprogramming.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots, stems and flowers; peaks at flower developmental stage 3; induced by wounding, oxidation, and light.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic rose embryos/shoots and tobacco flowers, overexpression elevates flavonoid-pathway transcripts.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYB-MYB interaction with RrMYB10 and interacts with bHLH AtEGL3; likely acts within a flavonoid regulatory complex.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive mutual activation with RrMYB10 as part of a flavonoid-mediated feedback loop responding to wounding and oxidation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Wounding; methyl viologen treatment; light; H2O2; dehydration
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Rosa rugosa RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA498442
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In tobacco petals, anthocyanin content decreased while PA increased, causing white/light pink flowers; possible flux competition among flavonoid branches.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for engineering high-PA, stress-tolerant ornamentals or crops; should be deployed with attention to possible anthocyanin/color tradeoffs and branch competition.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: When coexpressed with RrMYB10, activation of some downstream genes was lower than with either MYB alone, suggesting context-dependent interference or altered complex behavior.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rose leaves; petals; shoots; somatic embryos; tobacco flowers
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant protection and enhanced tolerance to wounding, oxidative stress, and dehydration.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis and stress-associated regulatory responses linked to PA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; mutual activation with RrMYB5; activates structural genes; likely indirect modulation of ABA signaling and antioxidant system
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: RrMYB5; RrDFR; RrLAR; RrANR; flavonoid structural genes including CHS, CHI, F3H, F3'H/F3OH, ANS; indirect effects on PP2Cs and antioxidant-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates flavonoid biosynthetic genes and RrMYB5; associated with up-regulation of antioxidant-related genes and down-regulation of PP2C ABA-signaling repressors in transgenic tobacco.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Principal wounding/oxidation-induced TT2-type regulator and upstream controller of RrANR; major switch linking PA production to stress tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases proanthocyanidin accumulation in rose and tobacco and also increases anthocyanin accumulation in tobacco; enhances oxidative and dehydration stress tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR induction analysis under wounding/MV/light; overexpression in rose and tobacco; RNA-seq in transgenic tobacco; transient expression in rose petals; dual-luciferase promoter assays; yeast two-hybrid; split luciferase complementation; metabolite profiling; stress assays.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RrMYB10 is a TT2-type MYB strongly induced by wounding and oxidation, with expression tracking PA accumulation. 2. It localizes to the nucleus, interacts with RrMYB5 and AtEGL3, mutually activates RrMYB5, and activates promoters of RrDFR, RrLAR and RrANR. 3. Overexpression in rose and tobacco enhances PA accumulation and stress tolerance, while transcriptomics show broad induction of flavonoid, antioxidant, and ABA-signaling related responses.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves and flowers; peaks at flower developmental stage 3; induced by wounding, oxidation, and light.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic rose embryos/shoots and tobacco flowers, overexpression elevates flavonoid-pathway genes; RNA-seq in tobacco reveals many DEGs in flavonoid, antioxidant, and hormone signaling pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with RrMYB5 and bHLH AtEGL3; functions in a flavonoid regulatory complex independently of requiring EGL3 for basic activation in some assays.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive mutual activation with RrMYB5 and participation in a flavonoid-mediated feedback loop under wounding/oxidation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Wounding; methyl viologen treatment; light; H2O2; dehydration
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Rosa rugosa RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA498442; Tobacco RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA498348
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can alter flower pigmentation through increased anthocyanin and PA; broad transcriptome rewiring may have pleiotropic effects, though explicit growth penalties were not reported.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulatory target for engineering PA-rich, stress-tolerant plants; suitable for stacking with downstream PA genes but color and hormone-network effects should be monitored.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states that transcriptomic similarity between RrMYB10 and RrANR transgenics further supports RrMYB10 as an upstream regulator of RrANR.
